title: Plastic waste generation by region and polymer
subtitle: Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).
caption:
Plastic waste generation by region and polymer, 2019

Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).

This stacked bar chart displays the regional composition of plastic waste generation for thirteen polymer categories in 2019, with each horizontal bar summing to 100% of the waste for that specific polymer type. The legend identifies ten distinct regions, including Europe, China, Asia (excluding China and India), the United States, and the Americas (excluding USA), among others. Across nearly all polymer types, the United States, represented by the teal segment on the far right, accounts for the largest single share of waste generation. For Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), the United States segment extends from roughly the 75% mark to the 100% mark, indicating it generates approximately one-quarter of the global waste for this polymer. A similar pattern appears in Road marking coatings, where the United States share is visibly the largest component, occupying the space from roughly 80% to 100%. In contrast, Europe, shown in blue on the left side of the bars, consistently represents a significant portion, typically ranging between 15% and 25% across categories like Polypropylene (PP) and Polystyrene (PS). China, depicted in maroon, generally occupies the second or third position in the stack, contributing a substantial share to High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE) and Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET). The remaining regions, including India, the Middle East & North Africa, and Sub-Saharan Africa, appear as narrower bands in the middle of the stacks, indicating smaller individual contributions to the total waste for each polymer. The data shows that waste generation for these specific plastic types is heavily concentrated in a few major economic regions, with the United States holding the dominant position in the majority of the listed categories.
